input.background: Use a statistical software package to conduct the test in Example 8.6. Analyze the results and interpret the $p$-value of the test.
input.question: Find the $p$-value of the test using the statistical software package.

output.answer: We entered the sample sizes ( $n_1$ and $n_2$ ) and number of successes ( $x_1$ and $x_2$ ) into Excel/XLSTAT and obtained the printout shown in Figure 8.16. The test statistic for this two-tailed test is shaded on the printout, as well as the observed significance level ( $p$-value).

meta_info.caption: Finding the Observed Significance Level of a Test for $p_1-p_2$
meta_info.section: 8.4 Comparing Two Population Proportions: Independent Sampling
